description:
## Policy change

Today (ir_typer.rs:238–242): *"the any-key only exists when the fn is
closure-reachable, entry-seeded, or genuinely needs the opaque-dispatch
fallback. Direct-call-only fns have no any-key."*

Flip the policy: **every reachable FnId gets an any-key spec.** Symmetric for
reachable conts: every cont fn gets an any-key entry in the same registry
(slot 0 = `Descr::any()`, captures = `Descr::any()`).

This is the floor. Narrowed specs continue to land above it as pure
optimization.

## Implementation sketch

In `type_module`, after the existing reachability-driven spec generation, walk
`module.fns` and:
- for each `f` with at least one registered spec (i.e. reachable), if the
  any-key (`vec![Descr::any(); arity]`) is not present, type `f` once more
  with that key and register.
- same for conts (`module.fns` includes conts; check by `kind` or by
  `is_cont_fn`).

`any_key_specs` index becomes total over reachable FnIds — currently partial.

## Tests

- Spec-table golden test: every reachable FnId in a fixture has an any-key entry.
- Unit test: a fn called only with `int` still gets an any-key spec registered.
- Existing `expected.specs` goldens get blessed with the new any-key entries.
- Spec count must not regress wildly: assert `n_specs_after <= 1.5 * n_specs_before` on `ast_eval` and `list_primitives` fixtures (sanity).

## Independent of (1) and (2). Blocks (4).
title: fz-aiz.3: mandatory any-key spec for every reachable FnId + Cont
